channel.title: TensorFeed.ai
channel.description: AI news, model tracking, and real-time AI ecosystem data for humans and agents.

item.title: These 5 Amazon Spring Sale deals are worth a second glance before they're gone soon
item.description: It's the last day of Amazon's Big Spring Sale, but there's still last chance savings on top gear from Dyson (including vacuums, hair tools, and more).

item.title: Amazon Spring Sale live blog 2026: Last day to score top Amazon deals
item.description: We're live-tracking the best Amazon Spring Sale deals over 60% off on home, tech, and more, during the final hours of the sale. Buy them before they're gone.

item.title: This minimalist smartwatch by one of my favorite tech companies is under $100 right now
item.description: On the final day of Amazon's Spring Sale, grab the CMF by Nothing Watch Pro 3 for $69.

item.title: You can now use ChatGPT with Apple’s CarPlay
item.description: ChatGPT is now accessible from your CarPlay dashboard if you have iOS 26.4 or newer and the latest version of the ChatGPT app, 9to5Mac reports. Apple's recently-launched iOS 26.4 update added support for "voice-based conversational apps" in CarPlay,...

item.title: I found the 3 best last minute tech deals under $100 during Amazon's Spring Sale
item.description: Improve your personal life with these great sub-$100 deals I've found during Amazon's Spring Sale. Act fast because the event ends soon.

item.title: The Apple Pencil alternative I use daily is on sale for $28 - but not for long
item.description: ESR's Geo Digital Pencil is on sale for under $30, but today's the last day to shop Amazon's Big Spring Sale.

item.title: The 5 most surprising things our readers bought on Amazon this week (No. 1 is weird)
item.description: ZDNET readers love random gadgets - and during Amazon's Spring Sale this past week, they picked up some interesting niche items that are still on sale.

item.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
item.description: No, the sky isn't falling, but Q Day is coming, and it won't be as expensive as thought.

item.title: The Galaxy S26’s photo app can sloppify your memories
item.description: Love to see the BACKSST BOYS at the Sphere. The Google Pixel 9 walked so that the Samsung Galaxy S26 could run. Google introduced AI editing tools to Photos slowly. It started with changes to the background - make the sky more blue, or remove crowds...

item.title: 7 WFH gadgets that improved my home office - Last chance for sale prices
item.description: As a remote worker, I'd recommend these WFH essentials even if they weren't marked down for Amazon's Spring Sale. Here's your last chance for discounts.

item.title: We're tracking the best last-minute tech deals under $50 during Amazon's Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon is full of great, affordable gadgets for your day-to-day, from speakers and chargers to streaming sticks and smart home gear. They're even cheaper now, during the retailer's annual sale - act fast.

item.title: The most popular Amazon Spring Sale deals may surprise you - and there's still time to shop
item.description: Amazon's Spring Sale ends today, but we've got the scoop on the top products ZDNET readers bought the most, from SSDs to flagship headphones.

item.title: This HP gaming laptop just dropped under $1,000 - a rarity during the RAM-pocalypse
item.description: The price of gaming laptops is through the roof, but right now at HP, you can snag the Victus 15 at a big discount.

item.title: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model
item.description: Veo 3.1 Lite is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API and for testing in Google AI Studio.

item.title: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: Compact Multimodal Intelligence for Enterprise Documents
Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
item.description: CERAWeek — dubbed the Davos of energy — is where policymakers, producers, technologists and financiers gather to discuss how the world powers itself next. NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled at the conference last week a new way forward — treating AI...

item.title: Art schools are being torn apart by AI
item.description: The curriculum at creative institutions is evolving to handle gen AI tools, and a lot of people aren’t happy about it. | Image by Cath Virginia / The Verge When my baby brother, a 3D modelling and animation student, talks to me about his projects...

item.title: Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative
item.description: In the early days of large language models (LLMs), we grew accustomed to massive 10x jumps in reasoning and coding capability with every new model iteration. Today, those jumps have flattened into incremental gains. The exception is...

item.title: You can order Grubhub and Uber Eats ‘conversationally’ with Alexa Plus
item.description: Amazon is giving you a new way to order food through Grubhub and Uber Eats with Alexa without having to endure an awkward exchange just to add fries. Amazon said the entire process is meant to be conversational, building your order in a similar...

item.title: AI Has Flooded All the Weather Apps
item.description: Weather forecasting has gotten a big boost from machine learning. How that translates into what users see can vary.

item.title: The Download: AI health tools and the Pentagon’s Anthropic culture war
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work? In the last few months alone, Microsoft,...

item.title: AI benchmarks are broken. Here’s what we need instead.
item.description: For decades, artificial intelligence has been evaluated through the question of whether machines outperform humans. From chess to advanced math, from coding to essay writing, the performance of AI models and applications is tested against that of...

item.title: Bitboard version of Tetris AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.26765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The efficiency of game engines and policy optimization algorithms is crucial for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents in complex sequential decision-making tasks, such as Tetris. Existing Tetris...

item.title: Multiverse: Language-Conditioned Multi-Game Level Blending via Shared Representation
item.description: arXiv:2603.26782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-level generation aims to translate natural language descriptions into structured game levels, enabling intuitive control over procedural content generation. While prior text-to-level generators...

item.title: Concerning Uncertainty -- A Systematic Survey of Uncertainty-Aware XAI
item.description: arXiv:2603.26838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper surveys uncertainty-aware explainable artificial intelligence (UAXAI), examining how uncertainty is incorporated into explanatory pipelines and how such methods are evaluated. Across the...

item.title: Neuro-Symbolic Learning for Predictive Process Monitoring via Two-Stage Logic Tensor Networks with Rule Pruning
item.description: arXiv:2603.26944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive modeling on sequential event data is critical for fraud detection and healthcare monitoring. Existing data-driven approaches learn correlations from historical data but fail to incorporate...

item.title: Compliance-Aware Predictive Process Monitoring: A Neuro-Symbolic Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.26948v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing approaches for predictive process monitoring are sub-symbolic, meaning that they learn correlations between descriptive features and a target feature fully based on data, e.g., predicting the...

item.title: Transparency as Architecture: Structural Compliance Gaps in EU AI Act Article 50 II
item.description: arXiv:2603.26983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Art. 50 II of the EU Artificial Intelligence Act mandates dual transparency for AI-generated content: outputs must be labeled in both human-understandable and machine-readable form for automated...

item.title: FormalProofBench: Can Models Write Graduate Level Math Proofs That Are Formally Verified?
item.description: arXiv:2603.26996v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present FormalProofBench, a private benchmark designed to evaluate whether AI models can produce formally verified mathematical proofs at the graduate level. Each task pairs a natural-language...

item.title: When Verification Hurts: Asymmetric Effects of Multi-Agent Feedback in Logic Proof Tutoring
item.description: arXiv:2603.27076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for automated tutoring, but their reliability in structured symbolic domains remains unclear. We study step-level feedback for propositional logic...

item.title: The Price of Meaning: Why Every Semantic Memory System Forgets
item.description: arXiv:2603.27116v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Every major AI memory system in production today organises information by meaning. That organisation enables generalisation, analogy, and conceptual retrieval -- but it comes at a price. We prove that...

item.title: MediHive: A Decentralized Agent Collective for Medical Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.27150v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized medical reasoning tasks, yet single-agent systems often falter on complex, interdisciplinary problems requiring robust handling of uncertainty and...

item.title: TRL v1.0: Post-Training Library Built to Move with the Field
There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?
item.description: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A couple of days earlier, Amazon had announced that...

item.title: The Pentagon’s culture war tactic against Anthropic has backfired
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Last Thursday, a California judge temporarily blocked the Pentagon from labeling Anthropic a supply chain risk...

item.title: Okta’s CEO is betting big on AI agent identity
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Todd McKinnon, who is co-founder and CEO of Okta, a platform that lets big companies manage security and identity across all the apps and services their employees use. Think of it like login management — actually, that’s a...

item.title: The Download: brainless human clones and the first uterus kept alive outside a body
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones After operating in secrecy for years, R3 Bio, a...

item.title: The IRS Wants Smarter Audits. Palantir Could Help Decide Who Gets Flagged
item.description: Documents show the tax agency is testing a Palantir tool to surface “highest-value” audit and investigation targets from a maze of legacy systems.

item.title: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
item.description: After operating in secrecy for years, a startup company called R3 Bio, in Richmond, California, suddenly shared details about its work last week—saying it had raised money to create nonsentient monkey “organ sacks” as an alternative to animal...

item.title: All the latest in AI ‘music’
item.description: People don’t like that they can’t identify AI music. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge AI has touched every part of the music industry, from sample sourcing and demo recording, to serving up digital liner notes and building playlists. There are...

item.title: Bluesky’s new app is an AI for customizing your feed
item.description: The latest app from the team behind Bluesky is Attie, an AI assistant that lets you build your own algorithm. At the Atmosphere conference, Bluesky's former CEO, Jay Graber, and CTO Paul Frazee, unveiled Attie, which is powered by Anthropic's Claude...

item.title: Suno leans into customization with v5.5
item.description: Slop yourself. | Image: Suno Suno just released one of its biggest updates yet with v5.5 of its AI music model. Where previous updates focused mostly on improving fidelity and creating more natural vocals, v5.5 is about giving users more control. It...

item.title: Why can’t TikTok identify AI generated ads when I can?
item.description: Samsung, like many companies using generative AI in their advertising, hasn’t placed an AI label on several videos shared through its TikTok accounts, and the fine print doesn’t always contain the answers. | Image by Samsung I've been struggling to...

item.title: Why OpenAI killed Sora
item.description: On Tuesday morning, everything was business as usual at OpenAI. By the end of the day, the company had announced that it would scrap its video-generation app, Sora, and reverse plans for video generation inside ChatGPT; it would wind down a $1...

item.title: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
item.description: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a restaurant kitchen. It is covered in flexible plastic...

item.title: AI Research Is Getting Harder to Separate From Geopolitics
item.description: A policy change announced by NeurIPS, the world’s leading AI research conference, drew widespread backlash from Chinese researchers this week and then was quickly reversed.

item.title: Apple Still Plans to Sell iPhones When It Turns 100
item.description: As the tech giant turns 50, WIRED spoke to executives about how they plan to win in the AI era.

item.title: The Download: the internet’s best weather app, and why people freeze their brains
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app The best snow-forecasting app for skiers isn’t a...

item.title: The Latest AI Documentary Asks: Just How Scared Should We Be?
item.description: The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist seeks the middle ground on a polarizing technology—and ends up letting tech execs like Sam Altman off the hook.

item.title: I Asked ChatGPT 500 Questions. Here Are the Ads I Saw Most Often
item.description: Ads are rolling out across the US on ChatGPT’s free tier. I asked OpenAI's bot 500 questions to see what these ads were like and how they related to my prompts.

item.title: Here’s why some people choose cryonics to store their bodies and brains after death
item.description: This week I reported on some rather unusual research that focuses on the brain of L. Stephen Coles. Coles was a gerontologist who died from pancreatic cancer in 2014. He had spent the latter part of his career specializing in human longevity. And...

item.title: Liberate your OpenClaw
Anthropic Supply-Chain-Risk Designation Halted by Judge
item.description: A judge temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s designation, clearing the way for Anthropic to keep doing business without the label starting next week.

item.title: Meet the Tech Reporters Using AI to Help Write and Edit Their Stories
item.description: Independent writers are using AI agents all throughout their reporting process. What’s the value of a human journalist, anyway?

item.title: Watch James Manyika talk AI and creativity with LL COOL J.
item.description: In the latest episode of our Dialogues on Technology and Society series, LL COOL J sits down with James Manyika.

item.title: Transform your headphones into a live personal translator on iOS.
item.description: Google Translate’s Live translate with headphones is officially arriving on iOS! And we're expanding the capability for both iOS and Android users to even more countries…

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
item.description: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is now available across Google products.

item.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
item.description: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a...

item.title: Search Live is expanding globally
item.description: We’re expanding Search Live globally, to all languages and locations where AI Mode is available.

item.title: Game On: Five New Titles Now Streaming on GeForce NOW
item.description: That gaming backlog won’t clear itself — GeForce NOW is here to help. Stream the latest titles straight from the cloud across a variety of devices. This week, five new titles are ready to play instantly in the cloud gaming platform’s library....

item.title: Senators Demand to Know How Much Energy Data Centers Use
item.description: In a letter sent Thursday morning, Elizabeth Warren and Josh Hawley press the Energy Information Agency to mandate annual electricity disclosure for data centers.

item.title: ‘She’s Never Going to Age’: Porn Stars Are Embracing AI Clones to Stay Forever Young
item.description: AI companion platforms like OhChat and SinfulX are offering adult creators digital twins, who are always at their peak and stay monetizing.

item.title: The Future of AI Is Open and Proprietary
item.description: AI is the defining technology of our time, quickly becoming core business infrastructure. It’s fueled by a diverse ecosystem of models: large and small, open and proprietary, generalist and specialist. This variety is essential for a future where...

item.title: Build with Lyria 3, our newest music generation model
item.description: Lyria 3 is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API and for testing in Google AI Studio.

item.title: Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more Google products
item.description: We are bringing Lyria 3 to the tools where professionals work and create every day.

item.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
item.description: Company warns entire industry to move off RSA and EC more quickly.

item.title: Blowing Off Steam: How Power-Flexible AI Factories Can Stabilize the Global Energy Grid
item.description: At the half-time whistle of the UEFA EURO 2020 round of 16 football match between England and Germany, millions of viewers stepped away from their screens in the U.K. to do the same thing at the same time — turn on their kettles. National Grid,...

item.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
item.description: Development houses: It's time to check your networks for infections.

item.title: Advancing Open Source AI, NVIDIA Donates Dynamic Resource Allocation Driver for GPUs to Kubernetes Community
item.description: Artificial intelligence has rapidly emerged as one of the most critical workloads in modern computing. For the vast majority of enterprises, this workload runs on Kubernetes, an open source platform that automates the deployment, scaling and...

item.title: A New Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents (EVA)
How Autonomous AI Agents Become Secure by Design With NVIDIA OpenShell
item.description: Autonomous agents mark a new inflection point in AI. Systems are no longer limited to generating responses or reasoning through tasks. They can take action: Agents can read files, use tools, write and run code, and execute workflows across...

item.title: Widely used Trivy scanner compromised in ongoing supply-chain attack
item.description: Admins: Sorry to say, but it's likely a rotate-your-secrets kind of weekend.

item.title: Build a Domain-Specific Embedding Model in Under a Day
NVIDIA GTC 2026: Live Updates on What’s Next in AI
item.description: Rolling coverage from San Jose, including NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s keynote, news highlights, live demos and on‑the‑ground color through March 19.

item.title: Cloud service providers ask EU regulator to reinstate VMware partner program
item.description: Broadcom says the group is misrepresenting market "realities."

item.title: Smooth Moves: 90 Frames-Per-Second Virtual Reality Arrives on GeForce NOW
item.description: It’s a double feature on GFN Thursday. This week, GeForce NOW offers smoother sights in virtual reality (VR) and a sprawling new land to conquer. Streaming at 90 frames per second (fps) comes to supported VR headsets. And Crimson Desert, which...

item.title: Federal cyber experts called Microsoft's cloud a "pile of shit," approved it anyway
item.description: One Microsoft product was approved despite years of concerns about its security.

item.title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
item.description: The latest open models and frameworks from NVIDIA bring together simulation, robot learning and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows.

item.title: Researchers disclose vulnerabilities in IP KVMs from four manufacturers
item.description: Internet-exposed devices that give BIOS-level access? What could possibly go wrong?

item.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
Bringing the power of Personal Intelligence to more people
item.description: We're expanding Personal Intelligence across AI Mode in Search, the Gemini app and Gemini in Chrome.

item.title: Our latest investment in open source security for the AI era
item.description: Google is making new investments, building new tools and developing code security to improve open source security.

item.title: Holotron-12B - High Throughput Computer Use Agent
Supply-chain attack using invisible code hits GitHub and other repositories
item.description: Unicode that's invisible to the human eye was largely abandoned—until attackers took notice.

item.title: The who, what, and why of the attack that has shut down Stryker's Windows network
item.description: Company says it doesn't know how long it will take to restore its Microsoft environment.

item.title: How AI is helping improve heart health in rural Australia
item.description: A new Google AI initiative aims to improve heart health outcomes for people living in remote Australian communities.

item.title: 14,000 routers are infected by malware that's highly resistant to takedowns
item.description: Most of the devices are made by Asus and are located in the US.

item.title: Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub
Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries
Ulysses Sequence Parallelism: Training with Million-Token Contexts
Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial...

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide....

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a...

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise...

item.title: Converge Bio raises $25M, backed by Bessemer and execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
item.description: AI drug discovery startup Converge Bio raised $25 million in a Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with additional backing from executives at Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz.

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and...

item.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four...

item.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds
item.description: When the creator of the world's most advanced coding agent speaks, Silicon Valley doesn't just listen — it takes notes. For the past week, the engineering community has been dissecting a thread on X from Boris Cherny, the creator and head of Claude...

item.title: Meta bought 1 GW of solar this week
item.description: The social media company inked three deals in the U.S. to power its data centers and offset its carbon footprint.

item.title: How one AI startup is helping rice farmers battle climate change
item.description: Mitti Labs is working with The Nature Conservancy to expand the use of climate-friendly rice farming practices in India. The startup uses its AI to verify reductions in methane emissions.

item.title: Harvard dropouts to launch ‘always on’ AI smart glasses that listen and record every conversation
item.description: After developing a facial-recognition app for Meta’s Ray-Ban glasses and doxing random people, two former Harvard students are now launching a startup that makes smart glasses with an always-on microphone.

item.title: Meta to add 100MW of solar power from US gear
item.description: The social media company is adding another tranche of solar to power a new AI data center in South Carolina.

item.title: Perplexity accused of scraping websites that explicitly blocked AI scraping
item.description: Internet giant Cloudflare says it detected Perplexity crawling and scraping websites, even after customers had added technical blocks telling Perplexity not to scrape their pages.

item.title: Obvio’s stop sign cameras use AI to root out unsafe drivers
item.description: American streets are incredibly dangerous for pedestrians. A San Carlos, California-based startup called Obvio thinks it can change that by installing cameras at stop signs -- a solution the founders also say won’t create a panopticon.

item.title: Breakneck data center growth challenges Microsoft’s sustainability goals
item.description: Microsoft's sustainability goals are imperiled by its push into AI and cloud services.

item.title: Gridcare thinks more than 100 GW of data center capacity is hiding in the grid
item.description: Gridcare raised $13.3 million for its data platform that finds underutilized capacity on the electrical grid.

item.title: Meta adds another 650 MW of solar power to its AI push
item.description: The company already has more than 12 gigawatts of capacity in its renewable power portfolio.
